source.title: The H1069Q mutation in ATP7B is associated with late and neurologic presentation in Wilson disease

description: ATP7B rs76151636 (H1069Q mutation) is the most common pathogenic variant causing Wilson disease in European and Caucasian populations. It results in impaired copper transport and neuropsychiatric presentation.

CC.phenotype: No Wilson disease from this variant
CC.recommendation: Standard health maintenance; family counseling if Wilson disease in family

CA.recommendation: No treatment needed; genetic counseling if planning pregnancy; confirm compound heterozygosity status with genetic testing

AC.recommendation: No treatment needed; genetic counseling if planning pregnancy; confirm compound heterozygosity status with genetic testing

AA.effect: Pathogenic H1069Q variant carrier; manifests late and with neurologic symptoms typically age 15-45
AA.recommendation: Initiate copper chelation therapy (penicillamine or trientine); maintain low-copper diet; regular serum ceruloplasmin and copper monitoring; neurologic and ophthalmology evaluation; zinc supplementation as maintenance therapy
